target.title: docs(browser): clarify networkidle wait profile support
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #80587 by removing the unqualified docs contradiction around `openclaw browser wait --load networkidle`.

The runtime supports `networkidle` for managed OpenClaw/raw CDP browser control, while existing-session / `user` profiles explicitly reject it. The docs now say that consistently in:

- `docs/tools/browser-control.md`
- `docs/tools/browser.md`
- `docs/cli/browser.md`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The docs should not simultaneously show `openclaw browser wait --load networkidle` as supported and say `wait --load networkidle` is unsupported without profile context. They should state that `networkidle` is supported on managed `openclaw` and raw/remote CDP profiles, but rejected by existing-session / `user` profiles.

**Real environment tested**: Local Linux source checkout on commit `50fbc87f04`, using the current docs and runtime source in the OpenClaw repo. This is a docs-only fix, validated against the actual browser CLI registration and existing-session guard code in the same checkout.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```sh
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
rg -n 'networkidle|existing-session|raw/remote CDP' \
  docs/tools/browser-control.md \
  docs/tools/browser.md \
  docs/cli/browser.md \
  extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/existing-session-limits.ts \
  extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts
pnpm docs:list
```

**Evidence after fix**:

The CLI action input still accepts `networkidle` generally:

```text
extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.ts:18:type BrowserWaitLoadState = "load" | "domcontentloaded" | "networkidle";
extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.form-wait-eval.ts:77:    .option("--load <load|domcontentloaded|networkidle>", "Wait for load state")
```

Existing-session runtime code and tests still reject `networkidle` for that profile class:

```text
extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/existing-session-limits.ts:22:    waitNetworkIdle: "existing-session wait does not support loadState=networkidle yet."
extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts:324:  it("fails closed for existing-session networkidle waits", async () => {
extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts:331:        body: { kind: "wait", loadState: "networkidle" },
extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts:338:    expect(String(body.error)).toContain("loadState=networkidle");
```

The three docs now use the same profile-scoped wording:

```text
docs/tools/browser-control.md:309:  - `openclaw browser wait --load networkidle`
docs/tools/browser-control.md:310:  - Supported for managed `openclaw` and raw/remote CDP profiles. The `user`
docs/tools/browser-control.md:311:    and `existing-session` profiles reject `networkidle`; use `--url`,
docs/tools/browser.md:741:- **Wait / upload / dialog** - `wait --url` supports exact, substring, and glob patterns. `wait --load networkidle` is supported on managed `openclaw` and raw/remote CDP profiles, but not on `existing-session` / `user` profiles.
docs/cli/browser.md:296:- `wait --load networkidle` is supported on managed `openclaw` and raw/remote
docs/cli/browser.md:297:  CDP profiles, but not on `existing-session` / `user` profiles
```

Docs checks completed:

```text
$ git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
# no output

$ pnpm docs:list
# completed successfully and listed the docs index
```

**Observed result after fix**: The docs no longer claim both unconditional support and unconditional non-support. They match the runtime behavior: managed/raw CDP paths can use `networkidle`; existing-session / `user` paths should use `--url`, `--text`, a selector, or `--fn` waits instead.

**What was not tested**: I did not launch a live browser for this docs-only change. The proof cross-checks the runtime source and existing-session regression test that define the behavior being documented, plus runs the docs listing command.
target.author: alix-007
